CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-04-2021 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Amy Silverstein, Esq. of Silverstein & Pomerantz LLP, 415.593.3502, for the Plaintiffs;
Atty. Daniel Robertson, Esq. of the Office of the Attorney General, 916.210.7516, for the Defendant.

DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA FRANCISE TAX BOARD'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Matter called. ARGUMENT is held.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court denies Ms. Silverstein's request to amend the complaint and adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant's demurrer is sustained without leave to amend.

The court declines Plaintiffs' request to take judicial notice of the legislative history. The language of the statutes is not ambiguous. "[T]he objective of statutory interpretation is to ascertain and effectuate legislative intent. In determining intent, we look first to the words themselves. When the language is clear and unambiguous, there is no need for construction." (Freeman v. Wal-Mart Stores, Inc. (2003) 111 Cal.App.4th 660, 665.)

(See Word minutes for the Court's complete ruling.)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-10-2021 Continued To May-04-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
